title: ERR Staff Portal
home_explainer_title: 🏠 Welcome to your Mutual Aid Portal home
home_explainer_intro: ✨ You’re on the home screen—your hub for opening the tools you use in the Mutual Aid workflow. Pick a card below or use the sidebar; both lead to the same places.
home_explainer_access: 🔒 The sidebar and the cards only show areas your account is allowed to use. If a colleague mentions a section you don’t see, your role or permissions may differ—that’s normal.
home_explainer_grant_desc: work with grant pools, cycles, and how funds are allocated across states.
home_explainer_f1_desc: submit and review F1 work plans (including from the ERR app and direct uploads).
home_explainer_f2_desc: review and approve F2 work plans, commitments, and allocations.
home_explainer_f3_desc: create and manage memorandums of understanding.
home_explainer_f4f5_desc: upload and review financial (F4) and program (F5) reports.
home_explainer_report_tracker_desc: see how F4 and F5 reporting is progressing.
home_explainer_project_mgmt_desc: review and manage project proposals and related workflows.
home_explainer_dashboard_desc: explore charts and summaries for mutual aid activity.
home_explainer_learnings_desc: read stories and learnings from projects on the map.
home_explainer_rooms_desc: manage room requests and approvals for ERR coordination spaces.
home_explainer_users_desc: invite, approve, and manage portal accounts (where your role allows).
home_explainer_surveys_desc: open evaluation surveys that apply to your role.
home_explainer_logout_desc: sign out safely when you’re done. You can use the help icon in the header anytime to reopen this guide.
room_management_desc: Manage and approve ERR room requests
user_management_desc: Manage and approve ERR staff accounts
project_management_desc: Review and manage ERR project proposals
f1_work_plans_desc: Manage and review F1 work plans from ERR App submissions and direct uploads
fsystem_select_type_desc: Choose the type of form you want to upload
fsystem_f1_desc: Upload F1 form and provide necessary details
f2_approvals_desc: Review and approve F2 workplans, adjust commitments and reassign allocations
report_tracker_desc: Track F4 and F5 report status
report_tracker_explainer_intro: One row per Mutual Aid project. You get F4/F5 status, amounts, and a quick read of progress—waiting, in review, or complete.
report_tracker_explainer_data_lead: When you open or refresh this page, the app loads the full tracker from the server in one step. Then the server:
report_tracker_explainer_data_bullets: Loads projects in approved, active, pending, or completed status, with ERR and donor information.
If your account is limited to certain states, only projects in those states are included.
Reads transfer date and exchange rate from MOU payment confirmations when available.
Builds F4 totals and dates from saved portal financial summaries and expense lines—not from raw historical-import-only summary rows.
Gets F5 presence and “F5 reported individuals” from saved program reports and reach records.
Uses planned activities and expense categories for “Main activity” and the sector filter.
Sets USD/SDG from the project plan: structured expenses when the project is portal-sourced, otherwise planned activities.
Sets “overdue” from the transfer date plus a fixed window, unless F4 and F5 are both far enough along for tracking.
report_tracker_explainer_scope_bullets: The note under the summary cards still applies: this tracker targets the Mutual Aid Portal workflow.
Changing status saves on the project record and only works for normal portal projects—not historical import rows.
report_tracker_explainer_features_bullet: Summary cards: average F4/F5 strength, tracker average, total USD, and project count—all for the filtered set.
Filters: donor, grant segment, F4/F5 status, state, date range, sectors covered. Chips can sync to the URL.
Wide table: scroll sideways to see every column.
F4 and F5 dropdowns: update stored statuses. The tracker bar averages the two status percentages.
Download CSV: exports the rows you have filtered.
Pagination: choose how many rows per page.
learnings_desc: View stories and learnings from Mutual Aid projects.
grant_management_desc: Manage and allocate grant funds to states
grant_management_explainer_intro: ✨ This page shows how grant funds are pooled, how distribution decisions land across states, and how those figures connect to Mutual Aid program activity.
gm_explainer_card_allocated: Sum of all distribution allocation line items from the program’s allocation tracking (the “included” pool across decisions).
gm_explainer_card_transferred: Sum of each grant call’s recorded activity amount—how much activity has been registered against grant rows.
gm_explainer_card_committed: Total locked-in spend: legacy totals from historical activity imports plus budget amounts from Mutual Aid Portal projects whose funding is committed.
gm_explainer_card_pending: Budget amounts from Mutual Aid Portal projects that are assigned to a grant or still awaiting vetting—not yet committed.
gm_explainer_card_remaining: Balance after subtracting committed and pending from Total Funds Transferred (same as the card subtitle: transferred − committed − pending).
grant_management_explainer_grants_table: Lists each grant call: ID, project name, start/end dates, status, total transferred (USD), sum activity amount, and transfer fees. Data comes from grant records linked to program finance tracking; donors are chosen from the active donors list.
grant_management_explainer_allocations: Each row groups a distribution decision (reference ID and date). Expand a row to see state-level splits—allocation amount and share of that decision. Data comes from the same distribution allocation tracking used for the summary cards.
grant_management_explainer_pool: Each row is a state. Use column headers to sort. Figures use the same building blocks as the cards above, broken down by state.
gm_explainer_pool_col_allocated: Allocated — distribution allocation amounts summed for that state.
gm_explainer_pool_col_pct: % of Total — that state’s share of the national allocated total.
gm_explainer_pool_col_historical: Historical — totals from legacy imported activity records (older spreadsheet-based activity), attributed by state.
gm_explainer_pool_col_committed: Committed — budget totals from Mutual Aid Portal projects (proposals managed in this portal) with committed funding.
gm_explainer_pool_col_pending: Pending — portal project budgets that are allocated or awaiting vetting but not yet committed.
gm_explainer_pool_col_remaining: Remaining — allocated minus historical, committed, and pending for that state.
grant_management_explainer_sources_lead: Figures on this page draw from four places:
grant_management_explainer_sources_1_tex: Distribution allocations and decision labels sync from the program’s allocation tracking.
grant_management_explainer_sources_2_tex: Rows in the grants table come from grant records linked to program finance tracking.
grant_management_explainer_sources_3_tex: Committed and pending amounts reflect project budgets—the proposals you work with in this portal.
grant_management_explainer_sources_4_tex: The Historical column (and part of the committed total in the summary) uses legacy imported activity: older spreadsheet-based data so past spend can sit alongside current portal work.
surveys_description: User feedback surveys for the evaluation (Feb–March 2026). Please complete the survey relevant to your role.
surveys_lohub_desc: For Localization Hub staff – time for analysis, data and tools, tracking, digital literacy (target 4/5).
surveys_err_desc: For ERR frontline volunteers – ease of submitting work plans and reports; visibility and communications (target 4/5).
surveys_link_placeholder: Survey link will be added here. Set NEXT_PUBLIC_SURVEY_LOHUB_URL or see docs/SURVEYS.md.
surveys_link_placeholder_err: Survey link will be added here. Set NEXT_PUBLIC_SURVEY_ERR_VOLUNTEER_URL or see docs/SURVEYS.md.
raise_ticket_intro: Report a bug or request an improvement. This opens an issue in the Mutual Aid Portal GitHub repository for the dev team.
raise_ticket_forbidden: You do not have permission to raise tickets. Ask an admin if you need access.
raise_ticket_rate_limited_seconds: Too many ticket submissions. Try again in {{count}} seconds.
raise_ticket_rate_limited_minutes: Too many ticket submissions. Try again in about {{count}} minutes.
raise_ticket_form_desc: Be specific enough that someone can reproduce or understand the request. Avoid sharing sensitive personal data.
raise_ticket_title_placeholder: Short summary of the problem or idea
raise_ticket_desc_placeholder: What happened, what you expected, steps to reproduce, browser/device if relevant…
raise_ticket_triage_note: Choose an issue type and priority to help triage.
raise_ticket_board_desc: Track status and progress for portal work directly in GitHub.
raise_ticket_dashboard_intro: Live reporting from the Mutual Aid Portal GitHub project board—Big Rocks, sprints, and open work.
raise_ticket_dashboard_note: Figures reflect board fields set during weekly triage; use the project board link above for full detail.
raise_ticket_label_hint: Matches labels in the Mutual-Aid-Portal repo so the issue is tagged correctly.
raise_ticket_priority_hint: Added to the issue description for context; project fields can be set during the weekly triage.
raise_ticket_chart_type_desc: Big Rock on the GitHub project board (set during weekly triage)
raise_ticket_chart_system_status_matrix_: Status by Type of Task · {{count}} tickets
raise_ticket_chart_no_task_types: No Type of Task values on the board for this Big Rock yet.
raise_ticket_chart_type_footer: Items on the GitHub project board grouped by the Big Rock field
raise_ticket_explainer_desc: Strategic objectives on the project board
raise_ticket_explainer_system_task_bulle: Data / Bug — data or functional defects
UI major — significant UI or feature changes
raise_ticket_explainer_expand_access_sum: Inclusive access for ERRs, partners, and states.
raise_ticket_sprint_intro: Work is organised in two-week sprint cycles on the GitHub project board. Progress is reviewed weekly and tasks move between the current and planned iterations.
raise_ticket_sprint_desc_default: Tasks in the current GitHub iteration
raise_ticket_sprint_no_active: No current iteration is configured on the project board.
raise_ticket_sprint_empty: No tasks are assigned to the current iteration yet.
raise_ticket_sprint_planned_empty: No tasks are assigned to the planned iteration yet.
raise_ticket_sprint_unscheduled_desc: Open tasks (Backlog, Ready, In progress) with no iteration · {{count}} tasks
raise_ticket_sprint_unscheduled_empty: All open tasks are assigned to an iteration.
raise_ticket_sprint_chart_level_empty: No open tasks on the board.
raise_ticket_sprint_chart_assignee_desc: Open tasks grouped by GitHub assignee
raise_ticket_sprint_chart_assignee_empty: No open assigned tasks on the board.
raise_ticket_sprint_chart_backlog_age_em: No backlog items with a created date.
raise_ticket_p0_unassigned_desc: Open P0 priority items with no GitHub assignee · {{count}} tasks
raise_ticket_p0_unassigned_empty: No open P0 tasks without an assignee.
raise_ticket_sprint_no_tasks: No sprint tasks to show on the project board yet.
dashboard_explainer_intro: This page blends three things: a hosted dashboard you can explore, a work-in-progress analytics area with charts filtered by date, and an embedded map. Use it for overview and exploration—figures here are summaries, not operational approvals.
dashboard_explainer_bullets: Looker Studio — embedded interactive dashboard (Google). You may be prompted to sign in if the embed requires it.
Work-in-progress charts — projects by donor, grants activity, and planned categories. Set the date filter to narrow the time range.
Map — geographic view in an embedded map panel; expand the section to load it.
stories_explainer_intro: Stories and highlights are built from program (F5) reports and project records already in the Mutual Aid portal. The page is for browsing, comparing, and sharing learnings—you do not upload or edit reports here. Use two complementary layouts: a horizontal carousel of story cards, a highlighted project panel with deeper detail when you pick a card, and a map that shows where work happens. When your interface language is English, some Arabic report text may be shown with an automatic English pass for readability.
stories_explainer_bullets: State vs theme — In state mode, narrow by Sudan state or stay on a whole-country view. In theme mode, pick one or more thematic tags to see projects that match.
URL and sharing — Your mode, state, and theme choices sit in the address bar, so you can copy a link that reopens the same slice for a colleague.
Carousel — Scroll the cards; click one to make it the highlighted story. The app may pick a starting card when the list loads.
Detail panel — Opens for the selected project: objectives, reach figures, and narrative pulled from saved program reporting when the data exists.
Map — Projects appear as points on the map; nearby points group into clusters. Click a cluster or point to open a short summary and jump to the same story in the carousel.
PDF export — Generates a snapshot of the current page content when you need to print or attach a static summary.
Read-only — Everything you see reflects what teams have already submitted elsewhere in the workflow; there are no edit controls on this screen.
room_explainer_intro: Coordinate Emergency Room (ERR) spaces: handle requests, keep active rooms up to date, and review inactive ones.
room_explainer_bullets: Sections below the title organize pending requests, active rooms, and inactive listings.
Use those sections to review listings and take the actions available on each row.
user_management_explainer_intro: Onboard and maintain portal accounts: review pending sign-ups, activate or suspend users, and browse active accounts.
user_management_explainer_bullets: Pending requests — approve or reject new accounts before they can use the portal.
Active users — search and manage accounts within your scope.
Access rights — optional links to fine-grained or bulk permission pages when those buttons appear above.
surveys_page_explainer_intro: Evaluation and feedback forms for the program. Each card opens an external survey (often hosted in Google Forms). Complete the survey that applies to you; links are configured by your deployment team.
surveys_page_explainer_bullets: Responses are collected by the form provider, not stored inside this portal.
If a button is missing, an administrator needs to set the public survey URL in the environment.

gm.total_included_desc: Funds allocated across all distribution decisions
gm.total_funds_allocated_desc: Funds allocated across all distribution decisions
gm.total_funds_transferred_desc: Total funds transferred into the grant pool
gm.submitted_desc: ERR F1s submitted and still under vetting (status = pending)
gm.approved_for_assignment_desc: Approved by vetting and waiting to be assigned to a grant (status = approved, funding = unassigned)
gm.assigned_desc: Assigned to a grant call and pending commitment (funding = allocated)
gm.committed_pipeline_desc: Finalized spending committed against the pool (funding = committed)
